Last week marked our 14th stay at The Proper Downtown Los Angeles.Occupying the former YWCA building, the hotel offers luxurious accommodations that incorporate original features from its past life. There's no mistaking this for your cookie-cutter Marriott: rooms are modern, well-appointed, and wonderfully funky. The Aesop toiletries are a particularly nice touch.The food and beverage program is top-notch. Breakfast at Cabrillo quickly became a daily tradition, and Cara Cara —the rooftop restaurant —offers the perfect vantage point for lunch or cocktails.However, it's the personal service and genuine hospitality that keep us coming back. From housekeeping to the GM, the Proper team has always welcomed us like family. They greet guests by name, work as a cohesive team, and are genuinely committed to delivering a consistently exceptional experience.

Beautifully designed. Great room. Shame the ground floor is so underutilised after breakfast.I’d recommend double checking your bill, though! I had a small unrecognised charge added after checking out and no response from the hotel 3 days later.
The staff so kind and accommodating during stay. Food was great and the hotel itself was so clean and unique. Very swanky vibes. Loved it!

I returned to this hotel after 11 months.  I have enjoyed many staycations here, especially in 2024, when the hotel's former ground floor restaurant, Caldo Verde, hosted Guest Chef/Cookbook dinner events almost monthly, when the hotel's food and beverage establishments were helmed by famed chef Suzanne Goin and her business partner/sommelier Caroline Styne.  Since my last visit, that culinary team has left, Caldo Verde stopped serving dinner completely, tried some live music events, and then was renamed Cabrillo, now serving breakfast only.  Cara Cara, the rooftop restaurant, remains open.  Dahlia, the bespoke cocktail and tea lounge, now just serves tea.  The menus at all sites have vestiges of chef Suzanne's influence, but also additions that are more global that the former Portuguese-forward menus.Change is also evident at Proper's neighbour hotel The Hoxton; their rooftop Stephanie Izard-led Cabra restaurant is now the Izard-less small plates Inanna.Despite my fond remembrances of things past, the Proper remains a gorgeous property with a great staff.  I saw a few familiar faces, and learned that others I remember were also still there.  Once again, the hotel acknowledged my past history and granted me early check in and a room upgrade.  The rooms are on the petite size, but oh-so-stylish (thanks to interior designer Kelly Wearstler) and pretty well-equipped.  I've always loved the scent of the Aesop amenities.  I once again had a room that ended in 02; the shower in these rooms do not have a curtain or glass partition, so I knew from past experience the floor gets quite wet.  But the hotel had partially solved the issue by lowering the water pressure and redirecting the shower head.  A curtain would be aesthetically unacceptable!  The hotel has a decent sized fitness centre and a small rooftop pool.During this stay, I tried the tea experience at Dahlia for the first time, see separate review.  I also had dinner at Cara Cara, which became dinner in the Cabrillo space with the Cara Cara menu because of the rainy weather.  I had the Cobb Salad, which was on the old menu (and apparently always will be).  It's unique because it has ingredients not often found in a Cobb like sweet potatoes, black beans and beets.  I also had a carrot/muhammara/beet hummus/grilled flatbread dish that was very Suzanne-ish, but don't remember from the past.  The herbed fries were excellent as always.  I looked at the breakfast menu, and it too is more expansive and perhaps more approachable than in the past.All in all, the Proper remains an excellent, unchanged DTLA hotel choice, and there's really nothing lacking in the new F&B offerings.

Good: Artistic, immaculate, stylishly understated designed rooms, friendly and professional staff.Bad: Property is fantastic. Surrounding area not so fantastic. I would have liked a couple of quality craft beers on tap at the bar rather than cans as the only option.
Good: Excellent variery of breakfast options. Portions were generous; well-presented and flavorful. Coffee was wonderful and service was professional, polite and non-obtrusive.

The Downtown LA Proper Hotel was an excellent find for a short business trip. Centrally located in downtown LA, I found it to have great amenities, including a small but well-equipped gym and good restaurants. The rooftop bar/restaurant offers impressive views and is a great place to end the day. The hotel's design is stunning, visually pleasing, and surprising. I would come back for a personal getaway.

Had an outstanding stay at LA Proper Hotel !  Highly recommend LA Proper Hotel for a luxurious, seamless stay in downtown Los AngelesThe check-in process was smooth and welcoming, making a great first impression. The property blends cozy warmth with contemporary style, making for a truly inviting and modern ambiance. The room was spacious and comfortable, and as an added perk, I received an upgrade through booking with the American Express Hotel Collection—which allowed for early check-in and late checkout options, making the experience even better.What truly stood out were the hotel's excellent dining options; both on-property restaurants offered memorable meals that made my stay even more special. All staff interactions were friendly, genuine, and professional, enhancing the overall sense of hospitality and care.

All of the staff were polite and helpful. The valet was also quick and good service - it was on par for price with other hotels in the area. The lobby and room was beautiful. the amenities in the room were up to date and functional. The cafe on the ground floor was delicious as well. The hotel advertises blackout blinds and soundproof rooms - I don't believe these are accurate. The blinds themselves were blackout but the gaps on either side of the blind let a lot of light it. Also they have added an extra window to minimize noise, but on Friday and Saturday nights there is a street food festival right next to the hotel that plays music so loud that it kept us up. You can hear general noise and also the blinds of your neighbours being pulled up and down but we didn't mind that. We were going to use the pool, but it was connected to the roof bar/restaurant and that put me off using it as it didnt feel very private.

Absolutely loved our stay at Downtown LA Proper Hotel.From the front desk and doormen to the restaurant staff, everyone was genuinely welcoming and professional. Both on-site restaurants made us feel like we were back on the East Coast — a bit old-fashioned in the best way, with great design and incredible food.Our room was surprisingly quiet, a rare find in DTLA, and the rooftop gets lively at night, adding to the charm. We’re pretty picky about food, but both restaurants here honestly exceeded our expectations — every dish was excellent.Didn’t get a chance to enter the pool (tiny but cute), but everything else was spot-on. Parking next door is about $30 overnight, right beside the valet and feels safe.Huge shout-out to the entire Proper Hotel crew — thank you for such a memorable stay.I truly love staying here and will definitely be back.

The hotel is stylish, clean, and beautifully designed. The service was excellent. The restaurant on the first floor was absolutely delicious!However, it does feel quite overpriced for what you get. Even with an Amex room upgrade, the room was still very small for the price point. The rooftop food was also expensive. But good thing is that if you are Amex platinum holder, they give you $100 food credits for restaurant and $60 credits for breakfast. That’s what I’m very appreciated for. The pool was tiny, located right next to the rooftop dining area, which makes it a bit awkward to use (no wonder it’s empty). The gym on the second floor was also small.To be honest, if I didn’t have Amex benefits, I wouldn’t choose to stay here! It’s a beautiful hotel with great service and aesthetics, but not quite worth the high nightly rate.

Reserved a room for a week, but had to check out early due to government shutdown. Management was understanding and didn't charge an early check-out fee, which I really appreciate. All staff encountered were very professional.Rooms are pleasant, and the hotel overall is clean. Gym is small-ish, but nice. Did not get to the pool, as I think you have to walk through the rooftop restaurant and bar to get to it, maybe?Location is fine, although two blocks to the east is where Skid Row starts. But, I walked through part of Skid Row and didn't have any problems. People are just people. I drove through Skid Row multiple times and didn't encounter any problems.  In the other three directions, you can easily walk to great restaurants and decent shopping.I hope to stay here again upon return to LA.
Good: Stylish hotel with a nice rooftop restaurant close to many things in downtown.Bad: The room was very small and parking isn't included.

Very nice hotel with a great rooftop bar, a nice little gym, and excellent and attentive service by everyone. Rooms are on the smaller side, even with upgrade, but they are nicely furnished. The bathrobes were torn, and we had to ask for slippers. The food was excellent, and we enjoyed our stay! Definitely one of the top hotels in downtown LA.

Proper is a great place to stay in Downtown Los Angeles. The decor and interior design gives off a boho and artsy vibe, which is both a treat for the eyes and senses. It’s a fun and playful imagination of what hotels can be, instead of stale and corporate designs that seem repetitive, bland and empty. Prosper has personality!There is incredible attention to detail that makes the Proper a curated experience from the moment you step in. The property felt so safe and well maintained. Security at the doors welcome and help you onto the property. The front desk concierge were welcoming and walk you through everything to make your stay as comfortable and enjoyable as possible such as the restaurants, complementary coffee/tea, rooftop pool, fitness center, and a thorough walk-through of the program perks.The rooms were clean, the beds and sheets were luxurious, the lighting was great and dimmable to set the vibes you’re looking for. The best part were the Aesop toiletries and products, yes, you read that right. Aesop. I have never been more relaxed after a night on the city. The robes are from Parachute, although light, were just fine and enjoyable.Restaurants were great. Cara Cara is the rooftop bar and restaurant with stunning views and the fresh and quality food. Caldo Verde is their ground floor restaurant, which is well decorated and feels bright, classy, and uplifting.Definitely consider Proper for your stay in Los Angeles.

Nicely renovated old hotel with comfortable bed, great shower and excellent staff. Lovely rooftop restaurant with great views of the city. Although downtown area is rundown, there are great restaurants nearby.
Trip and hotel were wonderful! The staff was incredibly attentive and amazing, and have to shout out Carlito who made my day every morning at breakfast!
A lovely experience overall: staff, food, room, comfort, location

I usually avoid Marriott Design properties since they’re not obligated to recognize elite perks like late checkout or complimentary breakfast. The Amex Fine Hotels of the World program solves that problem. Early check in, 4 PM checkout, $30 complimentary breakfast per person (max of 2) and a $100 property credit were granted. With that in mind anyone can feel elite at the Proper. The “pool” is really more of an opportunity for a plunge. It’s so small and shallow that there’s not much swimming to be had. We didn’t have any problems securing lounge chairs on a Sunday and Monday. The food and beverages at Cara Cara were superb. Be aware of a 6% addendum for employee health insurance. The veggie omelette at Caldo Verde was the best I’ve ever eaten. Thankfully housekeepers did not start knocking on our door before 4 PM as is the case at other Marriott family properties. There’s not much to recommend the South Park neighborhood in DTLA beyond this hotel.
